extra.reasoning:
The only transition that can set sent:=true while opted_out is true is t_resume_glitch, which is guarded by (opted_out and acc == 14). So a violation requires reaching acc==14 via step events, then optout, then send_attempt.

Each step event applies acc := (3*acc + c) mod 32 (the nested if-then-else just subtracts 32 up to 3 times; max input 3*31+31=124 needs at most 3 subtractions, so it correctly computes mod 32; verified all 32 inputs land in [0,31]). Constants: step_0 c=31, step_1 c=7, step_2 c=23.

I ran an exhaustive BFS over the acc state space (only 32 possible values, so the full reachable set is found regardless of the 34-event budget). Starting from acc=0, the reachable acc values are exactly {0,3,4,7,8,11,12,15,16,19,20,23,24,27,28,31} (16 values). acc=14 is NOT in this set.

There is a clean invariant explaining this: the constants 31, 7, 23 are all congruent to 3 (mod 4), and the multiplier 3 is -1 (mod 4). From acc=0, every reachable value is congruent to 0 or 3 (mod 4). The target 14 is congruent to 2 (mod 4), hence unreachable.

Since acc==14 is never reachable, t_resume_glitch is never enabled. After optout, every send_attempt matches t_send_blocked (opted_out and acc!=14), setting sent:=false. Therefore globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. The property holds; no counterexample exists.
